file_analyses.file_purpose: Base class module providing foundational Appium and Selenium WebDriver interaction methods for the entire NPI_Framework. Contains HPSmartBasicActions which is the parent class inherited by all driver modules (SmartDriver, UssDriver, UpdDriver, etc.) and page objects (Scan, Shortcuts, PrintDocuments, etc.), providing core element interaction methods like click_element, element_displayed, wait_for_object, send_keys_to_element, scroll operations, and window switching. Also contains HPSmartWebPageActions for web-based interactions used by Selenium-driven page objects for HP Smart web portal and SMB portal automation.

imports.import_statement: from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as EC
from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait as wd

classes.class_purpose: The foundational base class for the entire NPI_Framework that provides core Appium and Selenium WebDriver interaction methods. Every driver module class (SmartDriver, UssDriver, UpdDriver, UfdDriver, PsaDriver, SupdDriver, KliteDriver) and every page object class (Scan, Shortcuts, PrintDocuments, PrintPhotos, MobileFax) inherits from this class. Provides reusable methods for clicking elements with WebDriverWait, checking element visibility, waiting for elements to appear, scrolling to elements, sending keys to input fields, switching between windows, and performing ActionChains-based interactions. This class is instantiated with either an Appium WebDriver instance (for HP Smart app automation) or a Selenium WebDriver instance (for web portal automation) or None (for uiautomation-based driver classes).
classes.how_to_instantiate: base = HPSmartBasicActions(driver=appium_driver) OR base = HPSmartBasicActions(driver=chrome_browser) OR base = HPSmartBasicActions(driver=None)

classes.class_purpose: Specialized base class for web-based page objects that interact with HP Smart web portal and SMB (Small and Medium Business) portal using Selenium WebDriver. Provides methods specifically designed for web element interactions including clicking elements by dynamic text using XPath, verifying toast messages, switching between browser tabs, scrolling elements into view, refreshing pages, and waiting for element visibility. Used by page objects like account_hp_smart_dashboard, ews, instant_ink, and any other web-based automation classes. Always instantiated with a Selenium Chrome WebDriver instance from the chrome_browser fixture.

locators.ui_element_description: Dynamic XPath template used by click_on_element_by_text method to locate span elements by their exact text content. The {text} placeholder is replaced at runtime with the target text string to create a specific locator.

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance for HP Smart app automation, Selenium WebDriver instance for web portal automation, or None for uiautomation-based driver classes that do not use WebDriver

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the HPSmartBasicActions base class by storing the provided driver parameter as self.driver. This driver can be an Appium WebDriver instance (from appium_driver fixture), a Selenium Chrome WebDriver instance (from chrome_browser fixture), or None (for driver classes like SmartDriver, UssDriver that use uiautomation/pyautogui internally). All child classes call this constructor via super().__init__(driver) to establish the driver reference that is used by all interaction methods like click_element, element_displayed, wait_for_object, etc.

parameters.name: locator
parameters.description: Appium or Selenium locator tuple in the format (AppiumBy.ACCESSIBILITY_ID, 'value') or (By.XPATH, '//path')

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds for the element to be present before clicking. Defaults to 30 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Clicks on an element identified by the provided Appium or Selenium locator tuple after waiting for it to be present in the DOM. Creates a WebDriverWait instance with the specified timeout, uses EC.presence_of_element_located to wait until the element appears, retrieves the element reference, calls the .click() method on it, and logs 'Element clicked successfully' using logging.info(). This is the most commonly used click method across the entire framework and is called by virtually every driver class and page object method that needs to click buttons, tabs, links, or any other clickable UI element.
functions.usage_example:
# From SmartDriver
self.click_element(self.basic_tab, timeout=30)
# From Scan page object
self.click_element(self.scan_button, timeout=self.timeout)

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds for the element to be present

functions.what_it_does: Alternative click method that attempts to click an element using two different strategies with fallback logic. First attempts to click using WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located (same as click_element). If that fails and raises an exception, logs 'Click using Appium WebDriver is failed' and sets clicked flag to False. Then attempts a second click strategy using driver.find_element directly without explicit wait and clicks the element. If the second attempt also fails, logs 'Click using appium is failed' using logging.error(). This method provides more resilient clicking when elements are difficult to interact with or when timing issues occur.
functions.usage_example:
# Used in CommonUtilities and page objects when click_element fails
self.click_me(self.sign_in, timeout=50)

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds. If None, uses self.basic_wait_time (120 seconds).

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if element is not displayed. If False, returns False instead of raising.

functions.what_it_does: Checks whether an element is displayed on the screen and returns a boolean result. First attempts to wait for the element using wait_for_object with the specified timeout (or basic_wait_time if timeout is None). If wait_for_object returns True, returns True immediately. If wait_for_object fails or returns False, attempts a fallback strategy by calling scroll_to_element to scroll the element into view, logs 'Element scrolled to successfully', and returns the result of elm.is_displayed(). If both strategies fail, logs 'Element not displayed' and returns False. This method is critical for verification steps in test scripts where you need to confirm an element is visible before proceeding.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds. Defaults to 40 seconds.

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if element is not found. If False, returns False instead of raising.

functions.what_it_does: Waits for an element to be present in the DOM using Selenium WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located. Creates a WebDriverWait instance with the specified timeout and waits until the element matching the locator appears in the page structure. Returns True if the element is found within the timeout period. If the element is not found and an exception is raised, catches the exception, logs 'Element not displayed' using logging.info(), and returns False. This is a pure wait method that does not perform any action on the element — it only confirms presence. Used extensively throughout the framework before performing actions on elements.

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if scrolling fails

functions.what_it_does: Scrolls the page or container until the specified element becomes visible and returns the element reference. Initializes element variable to None and enters a while loop that continues until element is not None. Inside the loop, attempts to find the element using self.driver.find_element(*locator). If find_element raises an exception (element not yet visible), recursively calls self.scroll_to_element(locator) to continue scrolling. Once the element is found, exits the loop and returns the element reference. Note: This implementation has a recursive call which could cause issues — the actual scrolling mechanism is not explicitly shown in the code, suggesting this may be an incomplete implementation or relies on implicit scrolling behavior.
functions.usage_example:
# Scroll to element before interacting
element = self.scroll_to_element(self.advanced_settings_button)
element.click()

parameters.name: locator
parameters.description: Appium or Selenium locator tuple for the input field

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: String value to type into the input field

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds for the element to be present

functions.what_it_does: Sends keyboard input to an input field element after waiting for it to be present, clearing any existing content, and waiting briefly. Wraps the entire operation in a try-except block. Creates a WebDriverWait instance with the specified timeout, waits for the element to be present using EC.presence_of_element_located, calls element.clear() to remove any pre-existing text, waits 1 second using time.sleep(1) to allow the clear operation to complete, calls element.send_keys(keys) to type the provided string into the field, and logs success message with the keys value using logging.info(). If any exception occurs, logs the error with the keys value and exception details using logging.error(). Used for filling username, password, search fields, and any other text input controls.

parameters.name: link_element
parameters.description: Locator tuple for the link or button that opens a new window

functions.what_it_does: Switches WebDriver focus from the main window to a newly opened child window after clicking a link that opens a new window. First captures the current window handle using self.driver.current_window_handle and logs it as the main window handle. Calls self.wait_for_object(link_element, timeout) to wait for the link element to be present, then calls self.click_me(link_element) to click it which triggers the new window to open. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) for the new window to fully open. Retrieves all window handles using self.driver.window_handles. Iterates through all handles and switches to the first handle that is not the main window handle using self.driver.switch_to.window(handle), logs the successful switch with the new handle value, and breaks out of the loop. After this method, all subsequent WebDriver commands operate on the child window.
functions.usage_example:
# Click link that opens new tab and switch to it
self.select_child_window(link_element=self.help_link, timeout=30)

functions.what_it_does: Closes the currently active child window that was opened during the test session. Calls self.driver.close() which closes the current browser window or tab that the WebDriver is focused on. After calling this method, the WebDriver focus remains on the closed window handle, so typically you need to switch back to the main window using driver.switch_to.window(driver.window_handles[0]) or similar. This method is used in cleanup steps after interacting with popup windows, new tabs, or child browser windows opened during test execution.
functions.usage_example:
# Close child window and switch back to main
self.close_child_window()
self.driver.switch_to.window(self.driver.window_handles[0])

functions.what_it_does: Waits for an element to be present and then checks whether it is enabled (clickable/interactable) and returns a boolean result. Wraps the entire operation in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located to wait for the element to appear in the DOM. Once the element is found, calls element.is_enabled() to check if the element is in an enabled state (not disabled or read-only). If enabled, logs 'The element is enabled.' and returns True. If not enabled, logs 'The element is disabled.' and returns False. If any exception occurs during the wait or check, prints the exception message and returns False. This method is critical for validation steps where you need to confirm a button or input field is actually interactable before attempting to click or type.

parameters.name: scroll_element
parameters.description: Locator tuple for the scrollable container element

parameters.name: target_element_xpath
parameters.description: Locator tuple for the target element to scroll to

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if scrolling fails

functions.what_it_does: Scrolls within a scrollable container element until a target element becomes visible and returns the target element reference. First calls self.wait_for_object(scroll_element) to ensure the scrollable container is present. Wraps the scrolling logic in a try-except block. Finds the scroll container element using self.driver.find_element(*scroll_element). Enters an infinite while True loop. Inside the loop, attempts to find the target element using self.driver.find_element(*target_element_xpath). If found, logs 'Target element found' and returns the target element reference. If not found (exception raised), executes JavaScript to scroll the container into view using self.driver.execute_script('arguments[0].scrollIntoView();', scroll_el), waits 1 second using time.sleep(1), and continues the loop. If any exception occurs and raise_error is True, logs the error and re-raises the exception. If raise_error is False, logs the error and returns None.
functions.usage_example:
# Scroll to element within a scrollable list
target = self.scroll_to_elements(scroll_element=self.printer_list, target_element_xpath=self.specific_printer)

functions.what_it_does: Waits for an element to be present and returns the WebElement object reference for further manipulation. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Calls self.wait_for_object(locator, timeout) to wait for the element to appear. Once present, calls self.driver.find_element(*locator) to retrieve the actual WebElement object and returns it. If any exception occurs during the wait or find operation, logs the failure message using logging.info() and calls assert False to fail the test immediately. This method is used when you need direct access to the WebElement object to call methods like .text, .get_attribute(), or perform complex interactions that are not covered by the standard click_element or send_keys_to_element methods.
functions.usage_example:
# Get element object to read its text
element = self.element_return_to_object(self.status_message, timeout=30)
status_text = element.text

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if element is not found. If False, returns False instead of raising.

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a WebElement object by locator without any explicit wait, returning the element if found or False if not found (when raise_error is False). Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Calls self.driver.find_element(locator[0], locator[1]) to find the element using the locator strategy (locator[0]) and locator value (locator[1]). If the element is found, returns the WebElement object. If any exception occurs and raise_error is False, logs a debug message with the exception type and returns False. This method is used when you want to check for element existence without waiting and without failing the test if the element is not present.
functions.usage_example:
# Check if optional element exists
element = self.get_web_element(self.optional_banner, raise_error=False)
if element:
    element.click()

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if click fails

functions.what_it_does: Clicks an element using Selenium ActionChains which provides more advanced interaction capabilities than the standard click method. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Creates an ActionChains instance with self.driver. Calls self.get_web_element(locator) to retrieve the element reference. Chains the move_to_element(elem) action to move the mouse cursor to the element, then chains the click() action, and finally calls perform() to execute the action chain. This method is useful for clicking elements that are not directly clickable with the standard click method, such as elements that require hover before click or elements that are partially obscured. If any exception occurs and raise_error is False, logs a debug message and returns False.
functions.usage_example:
# Click element using ActionChains for better reliability
self.click_me_using_action_chains(self.dropdown_option, raise_error=False)

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Selenium Chrome WebDriver instance from chrome_browser fixture

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the HPSmartWebPageActions class by storing the provided driver parameter as self.driver. This driver should always be a Selenium Chrome WebDriver instance obtained from the chrome_browser pytest fixture. All web-based page objects that interact with HP Smart web portal, SMB portal, EWS web interface, or Instant Ink web pages instantiate this class or inherit from it to access web-specific interaction methods like click_on_element_by_text, verify_toast_message, select_tab_by_index, etc.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Exact text content of the span element to click

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds. Defaults to 45 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Clicks on a web element by locating it using its exact text content within a span tag. Wraps the entire operation in a try-except block. Waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5) at the start. Logs the attempt with the text value. Constructs a dynamic XPath locator by formatting self.dynamic_xpath template with the provided text parameter to create (By.XPATH, "//span[text()='{text}']"). Logs the generated XPath. Uses WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located to wait for the element to be present. Executes JavaScript to scroll the element into view using self.driver.execute_script('arguments[0].scrollIntoView(true);', element). Waits 2 seconds for scrolling animation to complete. Uses WebDriverWait with EC.element_to_be_clickable to ensure the element is clickable, then calls .click() on it. Logs success message. If any exception occurs, logs the error with the text value, saves a screenshot to screenshots/ folder with filename containing the text, and re-raises the exception.
functions.usage_example:
# Click on 'Printers' menu item by text
web_actions.click_on_element_by_text(text='Printers', timeout=60)

parameters.name: expected_message
parameters.description: Expected text content that should appear in the toast notification

parameters.name: toast_locator
parameters.description: Selenium locator tuple for the toast message element

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds for the toast to appear

functions.what_it_does: Waits for a toast notification message to appear on the web page, verifies that it contains the expected text, and returns the actual message text. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located to wait for the toast element to appear. Retrieves the toast element's text using toast_message.text and stores it as actual_message. Asserts that expected_message is contained within actual_message using assert expected_message in actual_message with a failure message showing both expected and actual values. Logs success message with the expected_message value. Returns the actual_message string. If any exception occurs, logs the error and re-raises the exception. This method is critical for validating success notifications, error messages, and status updates in web-based workflows.
functions.usage_example:
# Verify success toast after saving settings
actual_msg = web_actions.verify_toast_message(
    expected_message='Settings saved successfully',
    toast_locator=(By.CLASS_NAME, 'toast-notification'),
    timeout=30
)

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: 1-based index of the browser tab to switch to (1 for first tab, 2 for second tab, etc.)

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if tab switch fails

functions.what_it_does: Switches the Selenium WebDriver focus to a specific browser tab by its 1-based position index. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Calls self.driver.switch_to.window(self.driver.window_handles[position - 1]) to switch to the tab at the specified position (subtracts 1 because window_handles is 0-indexed but position parameter is 1-indexed). If any exception occurs and raise_error is False, logs a debug message with the exception type and returns False. This method is used when multiple browser tabs are open during a test and you need to switch between them to interact with different pages.

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if element is not visible

functions.what_it_does: Waits for an element to become visible on the web page (not just present in DOM, but actually visible to the user) and returns the element reference if visible or False if not visible. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with EC.visibility_of_element_located which checks both that the element is present in the DOM and that it has height and width greater than 0 (actually rendered and visible). If the element becomes visible within the timeout, returns the element reference. If the timeout expires or any exception occurs, logs an error message, prints a message with the locator and timeout values, and returns False. This method is more strict than wait_for_object because it requires the element to be actually visible, not just present in the page structure.
functions.usage_example:
# Wait for modal dialog to be visible
modal = web_actions.wait_for_element_visible(self.confirmation_modal, timeout=30)
if modal:
    self.click_element(self.confirm_button)

functions.what_it_does: Closes the currently active browser tab and automatically switches focus back to the first tab (main tab). Calls self.driver.close() to close the current tab. Then calls self.driver.switch_to.window(self.driver.window_handles[0]) to switch focus to the first tab in the window_handles list (index 0). This method is used in cleanup steps after opening and interacting with a new browser tab, ensuring that the WebDriver focus returns to the main application tab for subsequent test steps.
functions.usage_example:
# Close help tab and return to main app tab
web_actions.close_current_opened_tab()

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if refresh fails

functions.what_it_does: Refreshes the current web page by reloading it from the server. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Calls self.driver.refresh() which is equivalent to pressing F5 or clicking the browser refresh button. If any exception occurs and raise_error is False, logs a debug message with the exception type and returns False. This method is used when you need to reload the page to see updated data, clear temporary states, or reset the page to its initial load state.
functions.usage_example:
# Refresh page to see updated printer status
web_actions.refresh_page()

functions.docstring: Click on a specific element on the Instant Ink page.
functions.what_it_does: Clicks on a web element using Selenium ActionChains for more reliable interaction with web elements that may require hover or precise positioning. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located to wait for the element to be present. Creates an ActionChains instance, chains move_to_element(elm) to move the mouse to the element, chains click() to perform the click, and calls perform() to execute the action chain. Logs success message with the locator value. If any exception occurs, logs the error with the locator value and re-raises the exception wrapped in a new Exception with a descriptive message. This method is specifically designed for web page interactions where standard click methods may fail due to element positioning or overlay issues.
functions.usage_example:
# Click on Instant Ink enrollment button
web_actions.click_on_element(self.enroll_button, timeout=45)

parameters.name: raise_error
parameters.description: Whether to raise an exception if scrolling fails

functions.docstring: Scrolls the specified web element into view.
functions.what_it_does: Scrolls the web page until the specified element is visible in the viewport using JavaScript execution. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Calls HPSmartBasicActions(self.driver).get_web_element(locator) to retrieve the element reference. Executes JavaScript code 'arguments[0].scrollIntoView(true);' using self.driver.execute_script() with the element as the argument, which scrolls the page so the element is at the top of the viewport. Logs success message with the locator value. If any exception occurs and raise_error is True, logs the error and re-raises it wrapped in a new Exception. If raise_error is False, logs a debug message and returns False. This method is essential for interacting with elements that are below the fold or in scrollable containers on web pages.
functions.usage_example:
# Scroll to footer element before clicking
web_actions.scroll_element_into_view(self.privacy_policy_link)
web_actions.click_on_element(self.privacy_policy_link)

file_analyses.analysis_notes: basic_actions.py is the most critical foundational file in the entire NPI_Framework. Every single driver module and page object class inherits from HPSmartBasicActions or HPSmartWebPageActions. HPSmartBasicActions provides the core Appium/Selenium interaction methods used by all Windows app automation (SmartDriver, UssDriver, etc.) and all Appium-based page objects (Scan, Shortcuts, etc.). HPSmartWebPageActions provides web-specific methods used by all Selenium-based page objects (EWS, InstantInk, account_hp_smart_dashboard). The framework uses a consistent pattern: driver modules and page objects inherit from these base classes via super().__init__(driver) and then call inherited methods like self.click_element(), self.element_displayed(), self.wait_for_object() throughout their own methods. The basic_wait_time constant (120 seconds) is the default timeout used across the framework when no explicit timeout is provided. The module-level keyboard = Controller() creates a pynput keyboard controller instance that is available for import by any module that needs to simulate keyboard input. This file contains NO locators of its own (except the dynamic_xpath template in HPSmartWebPageActions) because it is purely a method library — all locators are defined in the child classes that inherit from these base classes.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Utility module containing CommonUtilities and CommonUtilitiesBrowser classes that provide reusable helper methods and complete end-to-end test workflows for HP Smart Windows app and HP Smart web portal. CommonUtilities inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and provides Appium-based methods for HP Smart app first-time setup, sign-in via Chrome browser automation using pynput keyboard, printer status checking and adding, print document workflows, and job status validation with toast notifications. CommonUtilitiesBrowser inherits from HPSmartBasicActions and provides Selenium-based methods for SMB portal login, Print on the Go enablement, and web portal navigation. Also contains standalone helper functions smb_portal_enable_potg, hp_smart_potg, and start_session for launching Appium server and executing complete test scenarios.

imports.import_statement: from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as EC
from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait as wd

classes.class_purpose: Utility class for HP Smart Windows app automation that provides reusable helper methods for common test workflows including first-time app setup, Chrome browser-based sign-in using pynput keyboard automation, printer status checking and automatic printer addition, print document submission, and print job status validation. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions to access core Appium interaction methods. Contains comprehensive Appium locators for all HP Smart app screens including welcome screen, sign-in page, dashboard, printers page, print documents page, and job status notifications. Provides complete end-to-end methods like first_screen(), signin_chrome(), printer_status_check_add_if_not_added(), print_documents(), and job_status_validation() that chain multiple actions together to accomplish complex test scenarios.

Utility class for HP Smart web portal and SMB (Small and Medium Business) portal automation using Selenium WebDriver. Inherits from HPSmartBasicActions to access core Selenium interaction methods. Provides methods for SMB portal login, Print on the Go feature enablement/verification, and web portal navigation. Contains Selenium By locators for all SMB portal elements including username/password fields, sign-in buttons, dashboard navigation, printer widgets, and Print on the Go toggle controls. The smb_portal_enable_potg method provides a complete workflow for logging into the SMB portal, navigating to Print Anywhere settings, and enabling the Print on the Go feature.

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance from appium_driver fixture

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the CommonUtilities class by calling the parent HPSmartBasicActions constructor with super().__init__(driver) to establish the Appium WebDriver reference. This class is always instantiated with an Appium driver from the appium_driver fixture because it provides methods for HP Smart Windows app automation using Appium locators and WebDriverWait-based interactions.

Handles the HP Smart app first-time launch welcome screen by clicking the Accept All button for privacy consent and then clicking the Sign In button to proceed to authentication. Calls self.click_element(self.accept_all_hp_smart, self.time_50) to click the consent button with a 50-second timeout, then calls self.click_element(self.sign_in, self.time_50) to click the Sign In button. This method is the first step in the hp_smart_potg end-to-end test workflow and must be called before signin_chrome().

Automates the HP account sign-in process in the Chrome browser window that opens when Sign In is clicked in the HP Smart app. Reads username and password from a config.ini file located two directories up from the current file using configparser. Constructs the config file path using os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), '..', '..', 'config.ini'). Checks if the file exists and raises FileNotFoundError if not. Reads the config file and raises KeyError if 'credentials' section is missing. Extracts username and password from config['credentials']. Waits 10 seconds for the browser to fully load. Uses pynput keyboard Controller to simulate keyboard input: presses Ctrl+A to select all text in the username field, presses Backspace to clear it, types the username string, waits 10 seconds, presses Enter to proceed to password screen, waits 15 seconds, types the password string, waits 15 seconds, presses Enter to submit login, waits 15 seconds for authentication to complete. Attempts to press Left arrow key and Enter to handle any additional prompts. If any exception occurs during the keyboard automation, prints the error message. This method uses pynput instead of Selenium because the Chrome browser window is not controlled by the Appium driver.

Checks if a printer is already added to the HP Smart app by looking for the 'Ready' status on the home page. If the Ready status is not found within basic_wait_time (120 seconds), assumes no printer is added and automatically initiates the Add Printer flow. Wraps the Ready status check in a try-except block using WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located(self.home_page_ready). If the exception is caught (printer not added), prints 'Printer not added' and attempts to click the Add Printer button. If Add Printer button is not found, prints 'Add Printer not visible'. Then waits for the Printers page title, waits for the IP address input field to appear, waits 30 seconds for printer discovery, and clicks the Remote connection option. Finally waits for the Ready status to appear with basic_wait_time timeout to confirm the printer was added successfully. If any step fails, prints the corresponding error message. This method provides automatic printer setup to ensure tests can proceed even if the printer is not pre-configured.

Executes a complete print document workflow in the HP Smart app from the home page through print job submission. Waits for the Ready status on the home page with time_30 timeout. Clicks the Print Documents tile. Waits 10 seconds for the Print Documents page to load. Attempts to click the Sample document option, then clicks the Print button. Waits for the Sample.pdf document name to appear with basic_wait_time timeout, waits 10 seconds, and clicks Print again to confirm. If any step fails, prints the corresponding error message. Waits for the Sending File status message, waits for the Cancel button to appear (indicating file is being sent), waits for the File Sent! notification with basic_wait_time timeout. Prints all window handles and titles for debugging. Waits for the OK button to appear and clicks the Job Status link. Waits 5 seconds. If the entire workflow fails, raises an exception with 'Print Job failed!' message. This method provides a complete end-to-end print job submission that can be used in any test requiring a print operation.

Validates the print job status by checking for either Job Printed success notification or Print Job Processing in-progress notification, and verifies the exact status message text. Waits 5 seconds, switches to the first window handle (main app window), waits 5 seconds. Attempts to wait for the Job Printed notification with basic_wait_time timeout. If found, clicks it, clicks Clear Notification, waits 5 seconds, clicks Close Print - HP Smart, and waits 5 seconds. If Job Printed is not found, catches the exception, clicks Print Job Processing notification, waits for the processing message 'Your file Sample.pdf is being processed. Please wait...', retrieves the element, gets its text using .text and .get_attribute('Name'), asserts that the Name attribute equals the expected job_processing_text constant, prints the text, and clicks Close Print - HP Smart. Then attempts to validate from the Activity notification panel by clicking Activity, clicking Print, switching to the first window handle. Attempts to wait for the processing message, retrieves it, gets the Name attribute, asserts it matches job_processing_text, prints 'Print Job is still in progress!', and clicks Close Print - HP Smart. If processing message is not found, waits for Job Printed notification, clicks it, waits for the success message 'Your file Sample.pdf has successfully printed', retrieves the element, prints 'Print Job is Successful!' with the element text, clicks Clear Notification, waits 5 seconds, and clicks Close Print - HP Smart. If the Activity panel cannot be opened, prints 'Notification Panel not opened'. This method provides comprehensive print job status validation covering both in-progress and completed states.

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the CommonUtilitiesBrowser class by storing the chrome_browser parameter as both self.chrome_browser and self.driver. This dual storage allows the class to use both self.chrome_browser for explicit browser-specific operations and self.driver for inherited HPSmartBasicActions methods that expect a driver attribute. This class is always instantiated with a Selenium Chrome WebDriver instance from the chrome_browser pytest fixture.

Executes a complete workflow to log into the HP SMB (Small and Medium Business) portal and enable the Print on the Go feature for a printer. Waits for the username field to be present, finds it using self.chrome_browser.find_element, sends the hardcoded username 'trilliumbasemp1@yopmail.com' to it. Waits for and clicks the 'Use Password' button. Waits for the password field, finds it, sends the hardcoded password 'srvt@123'. Waits for and clicks the Sign In button. Waits 45 seconds for the dashboard to load. Waits for the Solutions dropdown menu to be present, clicks it, waits 10 seconds. Waits for the Print Anywhere option within the dropdown, clicks it, waits 10 seconds. Attempts to wait for the Print on the Go toggle in the enabled/true state with basic_wait_time_medium timeout. If found, returns True indicating the feature is already enabled. If not found (exception caught), prints 'Print on the go not enabled', waits for the toggle in the disabled/false state, and clicks it to enable. Waits 10 seconds, finds the HP Privacy link element, executes JavaScript to scroll to the bottom of the element using scrollTop = scrollHeight, waits 10 seconds. Attempts to wait for the toggle in the enabled/true state again. If found, returns True. If still not found, prints 'Print on the go not enabled' and returns False. This method provides a complete SMB portal automation workflow for Print on the Go feature management.

Clicks the Accept All button for cookie consent on the HP Smart web portal or SMB portal. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located to wait for the accept_all button (By.ID, 'onetrust-accept-btn-handler') to be present, then calls .click() on it. Logs success message. If any exception occurs, logs the error and re-raises the exception. This method is typically called at the beginning of web portal test workflows to dismiss the cookie consent banner.

Enters the username into the HP account login form on the web portal. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Waits for the sign_in_link to be clickable using WebDriverWait with EC.element_to_be_clickable, clicks it to open the login form, and logs 'Sign-in button clicked successfully'. Waits for the username field to be present, calls .send_keys(user_name) to type the username. Calls self.click_element(self.use_password) to click the submit button that proceeds to the password entry step. If any exception occurs, logs the error, saves a screenshot to screenshots/login_error.png, and re-raises the exception. This method is part of the web portal login workflow.

Enters the password into the HP account login form and submits the login. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Waits for the password field to be present using WebDriverWait with EC.presence_of_element_located, calls .send_keys(pass_word) to type the password. Calls self.click_element(self.sign_in) to click the Sign In button that submits the login credentials. Logs 'Login successful'. If any exception occurs, logs the error, saves a screenshot to screenshots/login_error.png, and re-raises the exception. This method completes the web portal login workflow started by enter_username.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: common_utils.py is a critical utility module that provides high-level reusable workflows for both HP Smart Windows app (via CommonUtilities with Appium) and HP Smart web portal (via CommonUtilitiesBrowser with Selenium). Both classes inherit from HPSmartBasicActions to access core interaction methods. CommonUtilities is ALWAYS instantiated with an Appium driver from the appium_driver fixture and provides complete end-to-end methods like first_screen(), signin_chrome(), printer_status_check_add_if_not_added(), print_documents(), and job_status_validation() that chain multiple low-level actions together. The signin_chrome() method is particularly notable because it uses pynput keyboard automation instead of Selenium to interact with the Chrome browser login window — this is necessary because the Chrome window is not controlled by the Appium driver. CommonUtilitiesBrowser is ALWAYS instantiated with a Selenium Chrome WebDriver from the chrome_browser fixture and provides SMB portal automation including login and Print on the Go enablement. The module also provides three standalone helper functions (smb_portal_enable_potg, hp_smart_potg, start_session) that can be imported and called directly from test scripts without instantiating the classes. The timeout constants (time_10, time_20, time_30, time_50, time_100) are used throughout the methods to provide consistent wait times. The job_processing_text and print_job_processing_text constants are used for assertion validation in job_status_validation(). This module is heavily used in end-to-end integration tests that require complete workflows rather than individual action steps.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object class for HP Embedded Web Server (EWS) web interface automation using Selenium WebDriver. Contains the EWS class which provides methods for accessing and interacting with the printer's built-in web management interface including opening EWS by IP address, handling SSL certificate warnings, entering admin PIN, navigating tabs and submenus, printing status reports, performing printer alignment and cleaning operations, configuring network settings including Wi-Fi setup and IP mode toggling (DHCP/Manual), and validating operation results. Uses Selenium By locators for all web elements and provides comprehensive workflows for printer management tasks accessible through the EWS web interface.

from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait as wd

classes.class_purpose: Page object class for HP Embedded Web Server (EWS) web interface automation. Provides comprehensive methods for accessing the printer's built-in web management interface by IP address, handling SSL certificate warnings in Chrome, authenticating with admin PIN, navigating the EWS tab structure (Home, Scan, Web Services, Network, Tools, Settings), expanding and selecting submenu items, printing various status reports, performing printer maintenance operations (alignment, cleaning), configuring network settings including Wi-Fi network selection and password entry, toggling between DHCP and Manual IP modes, and validating operation results through success messages and status indicators. Does NOT inherit from any base class — instantiated directly with a Selenium Chrome WebDriver instance. Uses Selenium By locators exclusively and WebDriverWait for all element interactions.

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the EWS page object by storing the chrome_browser parameter as self.driver and creating a WebDriverWait instance with 100-second timeout stored as self.wait. This class does NOT inherit from HPSmartBasicActions or any other base class — it is a standalone page object that uses Selenium WebDriver directly. All EWS automation requires a Selenium Chrome WebDriver instance from the chrome_browser pytest fixture.

Opens the HP Embedded Web Server (EWS) interface by navigating to the printer's IP address in the Chrome browser and handles the SSL certificate warning. Constructs the URL as f'http://{ip_address}/' and calls self.driver.get(url) to navigate to it. Logs the opened URL. Wraps the SSL warning handling in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with wait_out timeout (30 seconds) to wait for the Advanced button to appear using EC.presence_of_element_located. Waits 1 second using time.sleep(1), then clicks the Advanced button. Logs success message. If the Advanced button is not found or any exception occurs, logs the error. This method must be called first before any other EWS interaction methods, followed by click_proceed_link to bypass the SSL warning.

Clicks the 'Proceed to [IP] (unsafe)' link on the Chrome SSL certificate warning page to bypass the security warning and access the EWS interface. Constructs the expected link text by formatting self.expected_text_template with the ip_address parameter. Constructs the XPath locator by formatting self.proceed_link_xpath_template with the ip_address. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Finds the link element using self.driver.find_element(By.XPATH, proceed_link_xpath). Checks if the link is displayed using link.is_displayed() and if its text matches the expected text using link.text.strip() == expected_text. If both conditions are true, clicks the link and prints success message. If the text does not match, prints a mismatch message with the found text. If any exception occurs, prints the error message. This method must be called immediately after open_ews to complete the EWS access workflow.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that the expected processing status messages appear during long-running EWS operations. Uses getattr to dynamically retrieve the appropriate processing message locator based on the text parameter: processing_print_text_xpath for 'print', processing_smear_clean_text_xpath for 'smear_clean', or processing_cart_clean_text_xpath for 'cart_clean'. Then waits for the 'This process might take several minutes.' warning message using self.several_minutes_text_xpath. Logs success message. This method ensures that the expected status messages appear in the correct sequence during operations like printing status reports, printer alignment, and printhead cleaning.

Waits 20 seconds using time.sleep(20) for the operation to complete. Uses self.wait with EC.visibility_of_element_located to wait for the green checkmark icon (self.green_right_mark_xpath). Logs success message with a checkmark emoji. Waits for the 'The print job has been sent.' message (self.print_job_sent_message_xpath) to appear. Logs the message text. Waits for the OK button (self.print_ok_button_xpath) to be clickable using EC.element_to_be_clickable. Clicks the OK button. Logs success message with a checkmark emoji. functions.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific top-level tab in the EWS interface by clicking the tab link. Constructs the tab XPath locator by formatting self.click_tab with the tab_name parameter to create (By.XPATH, "//a[@id='top-cat-{tab_name}']"). Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with wait_out timeout (30 seconds) and EC.presence_of_element_located to wait for the tab element. Clicks the tab element. Logs success message with the tab name. If any exception occurs, logs the error with the tab name. This method is the primary navigation method for switching between major EWS sections.

parameters.name: pin
parameters.description: Admin PIN code for the printer (typically from config.constants.ews_pin)

functions.docstring:
Enters a PIN for a specific tab in the EWS interface.
Args:
    pin (str): The PIN to enter.
functions.what_it_does: Enters the admin PIN to access protected EWS settings and handles the change password popup if it appears. Waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5). Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with wait_out timeout (30 seconds) and EC.visibility_of_element_located to wait for the PIN input field (self.pin_input_xpath). Logs success message. Clicks the PIN input field. Sends the pin string to the field using pin_input.send_keys(pin). Logs success message with the pin value. Waits for the Submit button (self.submit_button_xpath) to be visible using basic_wait_time timeout (60 seconds). Clicks the Submit button. Logs success message. Uses uiautomation to handle the change password popup: calls auto.GetRootControl() to get the root UI element, finds the OK button using dialog.ButtonControl(Name='OK'), checks if it exists with a 3-second timeout using ok_button.Exists(3, 1), and clicks it if found using ok_button.Click(). If the OK button is not found, prints a message. If any exception occurs, logs the error and calls assert False to fail the test. This method must be called after navigating to a protected EWS tab that requires PIN authentication.
functions.usage_example:
from config.constants import ews_pin
ews.select_tab_by_id(tab_name='Network')
ews.enter_pin_for_printer(pin=ews_pin)

parameters.name: submenu_name
parameters.description: Name of the submenu section to expand (e.g., 'Wireless', 'IPv4 Configuration')

functions.what_it_does: Expands a collapsed submenu section in the EWS left navigation menu if it is not already expanded. Constructs the submenu locator by formatting self.submenu_xpath with the submenu_name parameter. Constructs the expand icon locator by formatting self.expand_icon_xpath with the submenu_name. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. First attempts to wait for the submenu element (already expanded state) using WebDriverWait with wait_out timeout (30 seconds) and EC.presence_of_element_located. If found, logs that the submenu is already expanded. If not found (exception caught), attempts to find and click the expand icon using WebDriverWait with wait_out timeout and EC.presence_of_element_located. Clicks the expand icon. Logs success message. If the expand icon is also not found, logs the error. This method ensures that a submenu section is expanded before attempting to select items within it.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific submenu item in the EWS left navigation menu after validating that it is in the expected items list. Constructs the submenu item XPath locator by formatting self.submenu_item_xpath with the item_name parameter. Checks if item_name is in the exp_submenu_items list. If yes, wraps the operation in a try-except block. Uses WebDriverWait with basic_wait_time timeout (60 seconds) and EC.visibility_of_element_located to wait for the submenu item to be visible. Clicks the submenu item. Logs success message with the item name. If any exception occurs, logs the error with the item name. This method should be called after expand_submenu to ensure the submenu section is expanded.

ews.expand_submenu(submenu_name='Wireless')

functions.what_it_does: Executes a complete workflow to toggle the printer's IP mode between DHCP and Manual IP, apply the change, and handle all confirmation dialogs. Wraps the entire operation in a try-except block. Calls self.get_ip_mode_radios(timeout) to retrieve the radio button elements. Calls self.toggle_ip_mode(dhcp_radio, manual_radio, timeout) to toggle the IP mode and stores the previous mode as selected_mode. If selected_mode is None (neither radio was selected), returns None. Calls self.click_apply(timeout) to apply the change. Calls self.wait_for_processing(self.basic_wait_time) to wait for the processing message. Calls self.handle_ok_dialog(timeout) to handle the confirmation dialog if it appears. If handle_ok_dialog returns True (dialog appeared), calls self.click_apply(timeout) again to re-apply, and calls self.wait_for_processing(timeout) again. Calls self.handle_success_dialog(timeout) to handle the final success message. Waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5). Returns the selected_mode string. If any exception occurs, prints the error and returns None. This method provides a complete end-to-end IP mode toggle workflow with all necessary error handling and confirmation dialog management.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: ews.py is a standalone page object class that does NOT inherit from HPSmartBasicActions or any other base class. It is instantiated directly with a Selenium Chrome WebDriver instance from the chrome_browser fixture. The EWS class provides comprehensive automation for the HP Embedded Web Server (EWS) web interface which is the printer's built-in web management portal accessible via the printer's IP address. All locators are Selenium By tuples (By.XPATH, By.ID) and many use dynamic templates with {} placeholders that are formatted at runtime with specific values (tab names, section names, IP addresses, Wi-Fi network names). The class provides complete workflows for: (1) Opening EWS and handling SSL certificate warnings (open_ews + click_proceed_link), (2) Authenticating with admin PIN (enter_pin_for_printer), (3) Navigating tabs and submenus (select_tab_by_id, expand_submenu, select_submenu_item), (4) Printing status reports (click_print_printer_status_report + validate_processing_sequence_msg + validate_print_printer_status), (5) Performing printer maintenance (click_print_align_clean_button + validate_print_status for alignment, validate_cartridge_cleaning for cleaning), (6) Configuring Wi-Fi (select_wifi_network + verify_wifi_network + is_wifi_connected), (7) Toggling IP modes (toggle_ip_mode_and_apply which chains get_ip_mode_radios + toggle_ip_mode + click_apply + wait_for_processing + handle_ok_dialog + handle_success_dialog). The class uses uiautomation in one method (enter_pin_for_printer) to handle a Windows popup dialog that appears outside the browser context. The timeout constants (wait_out=30, basic_wait_time=60, medium_wait_time=120) are used throughout the methods. This class is critical for any test that requires printer configuration or management tasks that cannot be performed through the HP Smart app.

If the window does not exist, raises an Exception with message 'Print window not found within timeout'. If the window exists, calls window.SetActive() to bring the window to the foreground and window.SetFocus() to give it keyboard focus. Returns the window control reference. functions.docstring:
Ensures a radio button is selected. If not selected, clicks to select.

Parameters:
    window: The parent window object containing the radio button.
    toggle_name: The Name of the radio button (e.g., "Grayscale using black only").
    timeout: Max wait time in seconds.
functions.what_it_does: Ensures that a specific radio button in the K Lite driver dialog is selected by checking its current state and clicking it if not selected. Finds the radio button control within the window using window.RadioButtonControl(Name=toggle_name). Checks if the radio button exists within the timeout period using radio_btn.Exists(timeout). If not found, raises an Exception with message 'Radio button '{toggle_name}' not found within {timeout}s'. Checks if the radio button is enabled using radio_btn.IsEnabled. If not enabled, raises an Exception with message 'Radio button '{toggle_name}' exists but is disabled'. Checks if the radio button is already selected using radio_btn.GetSelectionItemPattern().IsSelected. If not selected, clicks the radio button using radio_btn.Click() and logs 'Selected radio button: {toggle_name}' with a checkmark emoji. If already selected, logs 'Radio button '{toggle_name}' is already selected' with an info emoji. This method provides safe radio button selection with validation and logging.
functions.usage_example:
window = klite.select_printing_document_options_from_k_lite_driver()
klite.ensure_toggle_button_enabled(window=window, toggle_name='Grayscale using black only', timeout=10)

Finds the two-sided printing combo box control within the window using window.ComboBoxControl(AutomationId='Part.JobDuplexAllDocumentsContiguously'). Checks if the dropdown exists within 5 seconds using dropdown.Exists(5). If found, calls dropdown.Select(option) to select the specified option from the dropdown and logs 'Selected option: {option}' with a checkmark emoji. If not found, logs 'Dropdown not found.' with an X emoji. This method provides two-sided printing configuration for the K Lite driver.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: klite_driver.py is a small driver module class (2,297 bytes, 3 methods, 0 class-level locators) that inherits from HPSmartBasicActions but does NOT use any of the inherited Appium or Selenium methods. It is ALWAYS instantiated with driver=None because it uses uiautomation library internally to interact with the HP DeskJet K Lite driver native Windows dialog. Unlike SmartDriver and UssDriver which have extensive class-level locator definitions, KliteDriver has NO class-level locators because all element identification is done dynamically using uiautomation WindowControl, RadioButtonControl, and ComboBoxControl with Name and AutomationId properties passed as parameters to the uiautomation methods. The class imports SmartDriver but does not appear to use it in the current implementation. The standard workflow for K Lite driver automation is: (1) Call select_printing_document_options_from_k_lite_driver to locate and activate the print window, (2) Call ensure_toggle_button_enabled to select radio buttons for color mode or other options, (3) Call two_sided_printing to configure duplex printing. This class is much simpler than SmartDriver and UssDriver because the K Lite driver has fewer configuration options and a simpler dialog structure.
